Curriculum → High-level design interview prep → Common product design patterns → File upload, processing, and distribution

Lesson 2 of 5

File upload, processing, and distribution

Media-heavy products require a pipeline for uploads, validation, durable storage, transformation, and fast global delivery.

25-35 min HLD flow Common product design patterns 4 guided stops

Study map

What this lesson helps you explain

This pattern combines object storage, asynchronous work, metadata stores, and CDN behavior in a concrete way.

Start here Frame the trade-off before diving into architecture. 1. Upload flow Avoid proxying large files through application servers2. Processing pipeline Emit events when uploads complete3. Serving and lifecycle Use signed URLs for private media

Guided walkthrough

Move through the lesson in a clear interview order

4 steps

Start with the framing, walk the concept in order, then use the right rail for prompts, pitfalls, and visual anchors.

1. Interview framing This pattern combines object storage, asynchronous work, metadata stores, and CDN behavior in a concrete way. 2. Upload flow Avoid proxying large files through application servers3. Processing pipeline Emit events when uploads complete4. Serving and lifecycle Use signed URLs for private media
1

Start with this framing

Interview framing

This pattern combines object storage, asynchronous work, metadata stores, and CDN behavior in a concrete way.

What to say first

  • Use direct uploads to object storage when files are large.
  • Process heavy transformations asynchronously.
  • Serve finished assets through a CDN.
2

Study section

Upload flow

Clients usually upload directly to object storage using signed URLs, then notify the application with metadata and processing requirements.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Avoid proxying large files through application servers
  • Validate file type and size early
  • Keep object keys immutable or versioned
3

Study section

Processing pipeline

Images, videos, and documents often need virus scanning, transcoding, thumbnail generation, or OCR in background workers.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Emit events when uploads complete
  • Store derived asset metadata separately
  • Retry and deduplicate processing tasks safely
4

Study section

Serving and lifecycle

Distribute content via CDN and attach lifecycle policies for retention, versioning, and deletion compliance.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Use signed URLs for private media
  • Distinguish hot serving from archival storage
  • Track egress and cache hit ratio

Checklist

Things to say clearly

  • Use direct uploads to object storage when files are large.
  • Process heavy transformations asynchronously.
  • Serve finished assets through a CDN.
  • Track lifecycle, privacy, and deletion requirements.

Common pitfalls

What to avoid

  • Sending all uploads through the core app tier.
  • Treating the object store as the metadata database.
  • Ignoring retention and signed-access requirements.

Interview prompts

Practice aloud

  • How would you design an avatar upload flow?
  • Where do thumbnails and transcoded variants fit?
  • Why should metadata and blobs usually live in different stores?

Related topics

Keep the thread going

Content delivery networks Networking, edge, and request routingQueues, streams, and background work Caching, asynchrony, and resilience

Simulation lab

Simulation lab: File upload, processing, and distribution

Use product-shaped traffic to explain where feeds, search, uploads, or messaging systems really bend. Apply it to file upload, processing, and distribution so the trade-offs feel concrete.

PRACTICE GUIDE

Explain the concept Explain the topic in interviewer-friendly language before diving into implementation details.

Explain the concept

Teach "File upload, processing, and distribution" back to an interviewer. Summarize what it is, when you would use it, and why it matters in a real system.

Goal

Explain the topic in interviewer-friendly language before diving into implementation details.

Guardrails

  • How would you design an avatar upload flow?
  • Upload flow
  • Processing pipeline
  • Use direct uploads to object storage when files are large.

Suggested Structure

  • Definition
  • When to use it
  • Benefits
  • Trade-offs
